problem.title: Your logistics is bleeding money through leaks
points: Your trucks leave at 85% when they could run at 99%.
Your team builds routes by hand, 3 hours a day.
Rejections surface only after the customer complains.
Every system speaks a different language and nothing reconciles.

platform.title: One platform.
platform.description: Visibility: see everything live, from vessel to last mile. Control: nothing slips by, lot by lot with real FEFO. Profitability: every trip pays off more, with routing and palletizing computed at once. All in real time, without replacing your ERP, it makes it stronger.

journey.eyebrow: At every point of the route
journey.title: From intake to signature,

reception.title: Intake
reception.body: Freight arrives by sea, air, road or rail. Geodot picks it up from the first point, whatever the mode.

warehouse.title: Warehouse
warehouse.body: Real-time inventory by lot, location and expiry. Pull a pallet and it tells you: you have 12 older ones in the back, grab those.

palletizing.title: Palletizing
palletizing.body: Palletizer and router work together: the truck leaves at 99%, not at 85% just in case. Fewer trips, same product.

route.title: In transit
route.body: It ships by any mode, with the ETA updating live. The AI learns by driver, customer and parcel count: the range drops from 30 to 10 minutes in three months.

delivery.title: Delivery
delivery.body: Digital proof of delivery: signature and photo on the phone, no paper. The driver closes it and the data returns instantly.

billing.title: Billing
billing.body: The cycle closes itself: what was delivered is ready to invoice and settle. Nothing is lost between the street and the office.

cases.title: Real numbers from operations like yours

items.result: Lower transport cost by going from 85% to 99% truck load
Per-customer shelf-life rule enforced at the scan: real FEFO, no surprise rejections

cta.title: See how much money is leaving on the table
cta.subtitle: 30 minutes with your own data and we'll show you exactly where your leaks are, and what they're costing.

wms.name: iWMS
wms.tagline: Real FEFO: it tells you which pallet is oldest, before it expires
wms.title: Warehouse Management
wms.subtitle: Your SAP knows you have stock. Geodot's iWMS knows which pallet, which lot and which shelf height it's on, and which one is oldest. Real FEFO by expiration date and shelf life per customer, so they stop rejecting your truck.

problem.title: Your warehouse loses money where no one is looking
points: Expired product hidden in the back: you find it when you can no longer sell it.
Picking from memory: the operator walks too far and builds orders with no guidance.
Phantom stock: the ERP says one thing, the floor says another.
Full-truck rejection: you ship product with little shelf life and it comes back.
No lot traceability: you don't know what you delivered to each customer.

stats.problem: Minimum shelf life a major retail chain requires
Look up a procedure with the AI assistant
Expired product hidden in the back

solution.title: A WMS that controls every pack, not just every pallet slot

steps.title: Real FEFO, not from memory
steps.description: Ships by expiration date. When you pull a pallet it warns you "you have 12 older pallets in this location, go grab those." The product hidden in the back disappears.

steps.title: Shelf life per customer
steps.description: A major retail chain requires delivery with 83%+ of shelf life. If a trip has a customer with that rule, the system applies it to the whole trip when you scan: "this one's old, grab a fresher one."

steps.title: Full traceability
steps.description: Scan the label and it tells you its status, where it's been and where it is now. A single material master: volume, weight, shelf life, quarantine and physical location in one data point.

steps.title: Optimized picking
steps.description: The same routing engine, applied inside the warehouse. Step-by-step guidance for the operator, and the expert's knowledge stays in the system instead of walking out the door.

metrics.label: Minimum shelf life the system guarantees per customer
Resolve a document query with the AI assistant, 24/7
Traceability by lot, pack and location
In production, not 6 to 12 months

cta.title: Control your warehouse down to the last pack
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll show you the iWMS running on your own SKUs.

tms.name: iTMS
tms.tagline: Every truck under control, owned or outsourced
tms.title: Transport Management
tms.subtitle: Transport is your biggest cost and your worst blind spot. The iTMS manages owned and outsourced fleets from a single screen: live tracking, documentation up to date, every drop validated, and costs attributed to each route and customer.

problem.title: Transport is your biggest cost and your worst blind spot
points: No visibility: you don't know where your trucks are right now.
Simulated drops: deliveries reported that never actually happened.
Expired documentation: permits and licenses that lapse with no warning.
Opaque costs: you can't tell what each route or customer really costs you.
Manual coordination: calls and WhatsApp to move every truck.

solution.title: Full control of your transport operation

steps.title: Live tracking
steps.description: Position by position on the map, geofences per customer, and automatic measurement of the real time each truck spends at each stop.

steps.title: Drop validation
steps.description: GPS isn't the dot on the map: every drop is validated against GPS. No more simulated loads and no more half-empty truck that goes out twice.

steps.title: Owned and outsourced fleet
steps.description: All in the same view, even when the truck belongs to a third party. Carrier portal with approval workflows and shared visibility.

steps.title: Documentation and costs
steps.description: An expired driver's license is a hard stop: the driver doesn't leave with that truck. Cost attributed by route, customer and kilometer, ready to settle.

metrics.label: Less transport cost, alongside the 85%→99% load jump
Visibility of owned and outsourced fleet
Drops validated against GPS
In production, not 6 to 12 months

cta.title: Put every truck under control, owned or outsourced
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and unify owned and outsourced fleets on a single screen.

paletizado.name: Smart Palletizing
paletizado.tagline: From 85% to 99% load, no safety margin left empty
paletizado.title: Smart
paletizado.subtitle: Others route first and palletize later. Geodot does both at once: we're the only ones who calculate palletizing and routing at the same time. We know what fits in each truck before loading it, so you load to 99% with no empty safety margin.

problem.title: Your trucks leave at 85%. That empty margin is money traveling empty
points: Safety margin: you load to 85% "just in case" it doesn't fit.
Rules in someone's head: the expert palletizer knows, the system doesn't.
Whoever goes out twice pays twice: fuel, labor and wear.
Returnable glass: the mandatory empty margin eats your truck capacity.
Picking with no guide: the operator doesn't know how to build each pallet.

stats.problem: Extra capacity from the returnable-glass rule
Fewer trucks per day with that same logic

solution.title: Palletizing that talks to routing, at the same time

steps.title: Simultaneous recalculation
steps.description: Every time you add an order to a route, the system recalculates palletizing instantly and tells you: this customer fits or doesn't, what goes on top, what goes underneath, how much of each.

steps.title: Trade craft in the system
steps.description: It knows glass goes on the bottom and what an empty safety margin is, with no need to explain. Stacking by product, family, customer, fragility and legal axle weight.

steps.title: The returnable-glass rule
steps.description: Take one glass customer off a truck and you fit ten more on the next one: +10% capacity and 4 to 5 fewer trucks per day.

steps.title: Visual build guides
steps.description: Which product goes in each layer and position, with a step-by-step sequence and operator validation against the guide, layer by layer.

metrics.label: Truck load, from 85% to 99%
Less transport cost
Extra capacity from the returnable-glass rule
Fewer per day with the same logic

cta.title: How many extra trucks are you paying for every week?
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll calculate your savings with simultaneous palletizing and routing.

torreControl.name: Control Tower
torreControl.tagline: You see everything, live. You fix it before it becomes a complaint
torreControl.title: Control Tower
torreControl.subtitle: A single dashboard to see, anticipate and act on your whole chain, from the warehouse to the last delivery. The AI learns from every trip: the ETA range drops from 30 to 10 minutes after a few months of operation.

problem.title: You operate blind until the problem has already blown up
points: Fragmented information: each system shows its own piece.
Late reaction: you find out about problems once they've already hit.
Weak ETAs: you give a wide window and the customer calls anyway.
Manual reports: hours building Excel for management.
Customers in the dark: they can't see the status of their order.

stats.problem: ETA range after a few months of operation
The AI learns by driver, customer and packages

solution.title: Your whole operation on a single screen

steps.title: Live dashboard
steps.description: Operations map with every truck, delivery status, active alerts and the day's KPIs against the average. You see everything, live.

steps.title: ETA that improves on its own
steps.description: The AI learns from every delivery: the ETA range drops from 30 to 20, to 15, to 10 minutes after about 3 months, by driver, by customer and by number of packages.

steps.title: Proactive alerts
steps.description: Stopped truck, delay at a customer, late route or compromised cold chain. By dashboard, email, WhatsApp or SMS, before it becomes a complaint.

steps.title: Customer portal
steps.description: The customer sees where their delivery is, live ETA, history and documents, with a non-intrusive push notification. Automatic daily, weekly and monthly reports.

metrics.label: ETA range, drops from 30 to 10 after a few months
For the AI to learn your operation
Visibility of the operation, on a single dashboard
In production, not 6 to 12 months

cta.title: What does every incident you catch too late cost you?
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and unify iWMS, iTMS, routing and palletizing on a single dashboard.

bebidas.name: Beverages
bebidas.tagline: Routing and palletizing at the same time, no slack left
bebidas.title: Beverages &
bebidas.subtitle: Your trucks leave at 85%. That empty space is money traveling for free. Geodot calculates routing and palletizing at the same time: it knows what fits the truck before setting the route, shifts glass customers between units, and builds every pallet with the glass on the bottom.

problem.title: Specific challenges we solve
points: Returnables: every truck reserves space for the empties coming back, and no one knows exactly how much.
Mixed palletizing: glass, PET, can and Tetra Pak, each with its own stacking rules (glass goes on the bottom).
15% safety margin: you load at 85% because you don't know if the pallet plan will fit.
BASIS integration: your core system lacks modern interfaces and stays siloed.
Specialized equipment (Tiger): not every SKU can be moved by full layer.

modules.title: Modules most used in your industry

items.result: Truck load factor and -8% transport cost by calculating routing and palletizing at the same time, with no slack left.
Truck capacity recovered by shifting returnable-glass customers between units, without touching the rest of the trip.
Fewer trucks per day with the same consolidation logic, freeing fleet for more routes.

cta.title: Load to 99, not to 85
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll show you, on your own operation, how to go from 85% to 99% with no slack left.

alimentos.name: Food & Dairy
alimentos.tagline: One platform: warehouse, cold chain and real FEFO in a single flow
alimentos.title: Food &
alimentos.subtitle: You find out about a temperature excursion when they reject the truck. Too late. Geodot connects plant, warehouse, routing and cold chain in one platform: real FEFO by expiry date, per-customer shelf-life rules, and an alert before the product is lost.

problem.title: Specific challenges we solve
points: Fragmented cold chain: product leaves at the right temperature but transport has no monitoring, and the rejection arrives after the damage is done.
Uncontrolled FEFO: oldest product doesn't always leave first because the warehouse doesn't enforce expiry dates automatically.
Per-customer shelf life: some customers require delivery with over 83% shelf life left, and a miss means the whole truck is rejected.
Recall: when a problem appears you don't know which customers received that batch.
Expired product hidden in the back: in a large warehouse, old pallets nobody sees get lost.

modules.title: Modules most used in your industry

items.result: When a pallet is pulled, the system flags that there are 12 older pallets in another location: expired product hidden in the back disappears.
Per-customer shelf-life rule applied to the whole trip at scan time: if a customer requires 83%+ shelf life, no old product gets loaded for them.
Separate appointments for chilled and dry product, with temperature sensors in trucks and rejection at receiving if out of range.

cta.title: Oldest out first, no excuses
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll show you real FEFO, per-customer shelf-life rules and cold chain in a single platform.

gobiernoResiduos.name: Government & Waste
gobiernoResiduos.tagline: AI-optimized routes and GPS that validates every drop-off
gobiernoResiduos.title: Government &
gobiernoResiduos.subtitle: Four in five residents feel waste is out of control. Routes designed a decade ago, full trucks that can't pick up more, and forgotten zones. Geodot optimizes routes, validates every drop-off against GPS, and puts the operation in front of citizens.

problem.title: Specific challenges we solve
points: Outdated routes: designed years ago while the city has grown and changed.
Simulated drop-offs: collections reported that never happened, with no way to validate them.
No visibility: you don't know where the trucks are or which points were actually serviced.
Uninformed residents: they don't know when the truck passes, so complaints pile up.
High costs: unnecessary kilometers and wasted fuel, every single day.

modules.title: Modules most used in your industry

items.result: Every drop-off validated against GPS: simulated collections end, and there's a record of which point was serviced and when.
Heat map and census of collection points by density and estimated tonnage, the basis for redesigning routes with AI.
Estimated potential CO₂ avoided through shorter routes in a collection project, alongside a target of up to -15% in km and fuel.

cta.title: Fewer miles, fewer complaints, drop-offs that actually happened
cta.subtitle: Book a meeting and we'll deliver a no-cost route assessment and collection-point census.

logistica3pl.name: 3PL Logistics
logistica3pl.tagline: Zero Label + iWMS: the data travels with the pallet
logistica3pl.title: Logistics Operators
logistica3pl.subtitle: Every client asks for a different report and you have no way to show it to them. Geodot starts with Zero Label and iWMS (no re-labeling, the data already travels with the pallet) and adds multi-client, routing and billing when you need them: land and expand on your operation.

problem.title: Specific challenges we solve
points: Manual re-labeling: at receiving, every label is reprinted and re-stuck by hand, a constant source of errors.
Assets without tracking: you don't know which site each forklift or crane is at, and your client is calling because they don't know either.
Manual documentation: usage logs, billing and reports are built in spreadsheets and there are always discrepancies at month-end close.
Every client wants their report: without a multi-client portal, showing each account its operation is a juggling act.
Load consolidation: orders from multiple clients to the same area, hard to combine by hand.

modules.title: Modules most used in your industry

items.result: No manual re-labeling at receiving: the data already travels with the pallet, ending reprint-and-restick errors.
Each account with its own visibility and reports in a 24/7 portal, without building a spreadsheet per client.
Real-time location and status of every rented equipment unit, with usage history by client and by shift.

cta.title: Start with Zero Label, grow with the whole platform
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll show you how to eliminate re-labeling and give each client a live view of their operation.

healthcare.name: Healthcare & Pharma
healthcare.tagline: Cold chain with alerts before the damage, not after
healthcare.title: Healthcare &
healthcare.subtitle: Vaccines, clinical samples and high-cost medications tolerate not one degree out of range. Geodot enters through the cold chain: end-to-end temperature traceability, an alert when the excursion happens (not when they reject the truck), and real FEFO so the closest-to-expiry product leaves first.

problem.title: Specific challenges we solve
points: Fragmented cold network: product leaves the central warehouse at 4 °C but the last mile has no monitoring, and that's where most excursions happen.
You find out late: without sensors in the truck, the excursion surfaces when the delivery is rejected.
Strict time windows: samples have a shelf life and transfers are measured in minutes, not hours.
Thousands of SKUs with expiry dates: mandatory FEFO, lots, controlled products and recall traceability.
Chain of custody: every movement must be logged with timestamp, temperature and owner for audits.

modules.title: Modules most used in your industry

items.result: Temperature sensors in chilled trucks and automatic rejection at receiving if product arrives out of range: the excursion shows up when it happens.
Dispatch by expiry date with an alert at scan time when an older pallet sits in another location: nothing expired stays hidden in the back.
Every movement logged with timestamp, temperature and owner, with continuous traceability from central warehouse to delivery point.

cta.title: Logistics that tolerates not one degree out of range
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll show you how to guarantee cold chain, real FEFO and chain of custody in a single platform.

recintosFiscales.name: Bonded Warehouses
recintosFiscales.tagline: Zero Label + iWMS for an auditable facility
recintosFiscales.title: Bonded &
recintosFiscales.subtitle: Without digital evidence, an ANAM audit is a gamble. Geodot enters through Zero Label and iWMS: no re-labeling, the data travels with the pallet, and every movement inside the facility is logged and auditable, with a document manager and control tower when you need them.

problem.title: Specific challenges we solve
points: Re-labeling at receiving: every pallet entering the facility is reprinted and re-stuck by hand, a source of errors before the authority.
Regulatory compliance: ANAM, IMMEX and OEA require integrated inventory, video and traceability, with remote access for the authority.
Goods in custody: every movement inside the facility must be logged and auditable.
Differentiated zones: hazardous, refrigerated and standard cargo coexist under different rules in the same warehouse.
Physical vs. document reconciliation: counting what comes in and out against what was declared, with evidence.

modules.title: Modules most used in your industry

items.result: Bonded warehouse digitized with iWMS and Zero Label: no re-labeling and pallet-level traceability across hazardous, refrigerated and standard zones.
Every movement inside the facility logged and available for inspection, with physical reconciliation against what was declared.
items.client: A facility in a port zone

items.result: Document query resolved by an AI assistant in under 2 minutes, 24/7, always on the current version.

cta.title: A facility auditable end to end
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll show you Zero Label, pallet-level traceability and the evidence ANAM, IMMEX and OEA require.

hero.title: Power up your ERP in 90 days, not
hero.subtitle: Your SAP knows you have stock. Geodot knows which pallet, which lot and which shelf height it's on, and loads your trucks from 85% to 99%. WMS, TMS, routing and palletizing on one platform: the router and the palletizer calculate at the same time, live. In production in 90 days, not 6 to 12 months.

modules.title: Modules that work as one
modules.description: We're not here to replace your SAP, we're here to reach where your SAP can't: pack, pallet, lot, location, palletizing. We integrate with SAP, SAP Business One, BASIS and Oracle through our own APIs, and add the one thing no one else does: routing and palletizing calculated together.

cta.title: Where is your money leaking out today?
cta.subtitle: Trucks at 85%, routes planned by hand, rejections you catch too late. Book a demo and we'll show you, in your own operation, how much you can recover.

hero.title: Logistics that understands your
hero.subtitle: Every sector enters through its own pain: bonded and 3PL through Zero Label, government through GPS, food through cold chain and FEFO, beverages through routing and palletizing. Geodot adapts to the operation you already run and grows from there.

industriesIndex.title: Six industries,
industriesIndex.subtitle: Pick your sector and find the right module to start with, plus how Geodot solves the concrete problems of your operation.

cta.title: Don't see your industry here?
cta.subtitle: If you move inventory, routes or trucks, Geodot adapts to your operation. Book a demo and we'll walk through your numbers.

hero.title: Numbers you can measure, not
hero.subtitle: Bottlers, fishing fleets and cold-chain operations already move more with Geodot. These are the real numbers, and we flag what's in production versus what we showed in a demo. Nothing invented.

list.title: The impact of Geodot in numbers

items.result: Went from loading trucks at 85% to 99% of capacity and cut transport cost by 8%. The returnable-glass rule added 10% capacity and took 4 to 5 trucks a day off the road.
23 vessels in production with telemetry and the Control Tower on a single screen. Real visibility of the operation at sea, not a lost dot on a map.
Routing went from about 3 hours to routing 5,000 orders in about 10 minutes. ETAs started in a 30-minute window and the AI tightened them to 10 in around 3 months.

studies.title: Three operations, three pain points, three results

items.challenge: Trucks leaving at 85% and routes built by hand every morning. Moving the same volume took 100 trucks when better loading needed 150. The union's safety margin ate real capacity.
items.solution: Geodot calculates palletizing and routing at the same time: it knows what fits in the truck before building the route. It shifted returnable-glass customers between trucks to free capacity without breaking the margin.
items.result: Truck load from 85% to 99% and 8% lower transport cost. The glass rule added 10% capacity and took 4 to 5 trucks a day off the road.

items.industry: Cold chain and traceability (shown in a demo)
items.challenge: Expired product hidden at the back of the warehouse and full-truck rejections because a customer demands delivery with over 83% shelf-life remaining. Traceability took days to answer a claim.
items.solution: Geodot applies real FEFO at scan time: if you pull a pallet, it warns there are 12 older ones in another location and sends you to fetch those. The customer's shelf-life rule applies to the whole trip, not just one pallet.
items.result: Expired product hidden at the back and full-truck rejections over shelf-life both disappear. The ETA window dropped from 30 to 10 minutes in around 3 months as the AI learned.

items.challenge: Goods under customs control tracked on spreadsheets and manual re-labeling at receiving, risking discrepancies and lost hours on every inspection.
items.solution: Geodot adds iWMS with Zero Label so the data travels with the pallet without reprinting labels, plus a document manager with an AI assistant that answers a procedure question in under 2 minutes, 24/7.
items.result: Zero manual re-labeling, every move logged in real time, and the operation kept audit-ready. The document lookup that used to take hours is resolved in under 2 minutes.

cta.title: Want numbers like these in your operation?
cta.subtitle: Book a demo and we'll show you, with your own data, where your invisible leaks are and how much you can save.

hero.title: Born from operations,
hero.subtitle: 40+ combined years moving trucks and 15+ with leading bottlers. We built Geodot because we've fought the same fires you do: no one has to explain to us what a safety margin is or why the glass goes on the bottom.

mission.title: Connecting every point of the supply chain

timeline.title: The road that brought us here

steps.label: On the dock
steps.title: A lifetime in the field
steps.body: More than 40 combined years moving trucks, building routes and grinding it out on the floor. We didn't read about logistics: we lived it shift after shift.

steps.label: With bottlers
steps.title: Where the glass goes on the bottom
steps.body: More than 15 years operating with leading bottlers taught us that every pallet, every degree and every kilometer counts. That's where we learned not to break what already works.

steps.label: Why Geodot
steps.title: The tools never added up
steps.body: One system routed but didn't palletize. The WMS didn't talk to the TMS. Tired of the juggling, we decided to build the platform we always wished we had in the operation.

steps.label: Across LATAM
steps.title: One platform, four countries
steps.body: We operate across Mexico, Argentina, Colombia and Ecuador, with real experience in beverages, food, government, pharma and bonded warehouses. We're still operations people, now with our own technology.

body: After more than 40 combined years in logistics, production and technology, we kept seeing the same problem: the tools never solved the whole operation. One system routed but didn't palletize. The WMS didn't talk to the TMS. And in the middle, operations teams juggling to make it all add up.

Geodot was born to end that. We built the first platform that unifies WMS, TMS, routing and palletizing in a single solution, where the routing engine and the palletizer work together, in real time, to load every truck to 99% and route by business value, not just kilometers.

We don't promise magic: we promise results you can measure. At a leading bottler, truck load went from 85% to 99% and transport cost dropped 8%. And we don't replace your ERP: we power it, integrating with SAP, BASIS and Oracle without breaking what already works.

We're a Mexican technology company operating across Mexico, Argentina, Colombia and Ecuador, with real experience in beverages, food, government, pharma and bonded warehouses. We imagine a world where every truck leaves at 100% and every product arrives fresh to the right customer. That's the world we're building, with you.

cta.title: Let's talk about your operation
cta.subtitle: Book a call and tell us your challenges. We want to understand your operation before we propose anything.

meta.title: Privacy Policy | Geodot
meta.description: How Geodot collects, uses, and protects the personal data you share through our website.

hero.title: Privacy
hero.subtitle: How we collect, use, and protect the personal data you share with Geodot through this site.

sections.title: 1. Who we are
body: Geodot is a logistics technology company. This policy describes how we handle the personal information we collect through our website. For any privacy questions, email us at hola@geodot.app.

sections.title: 2. Data we collect
body: Information you voluntarily provide through the contact form: name, email address, company, and the message you write.

Technical and usage data collected automatically through web analytics tools: pages visited, device type, browser, and aggregate browsing data. We do not use this information to identify you personally.

sections.title: 3. How we use your data
body: To respond to your inquiries, coordinate demos, and follow up on commercial conversations.
To understand how the site is used and improve its content and performance.
We do not sell or rent your personal data to third parties.

sections.title: 4. Service providers and processors
body: To send and manage contact-form messages we use Resend (resend.com) as our email processor. Resend handles the data on our behalf, under its own terms and security measures.
For web analytics we may use measurement providers that process aggregate and technical data. These providers act as processors under their respective policies.

sections.title: 5. Retention
body: We keep contact-form data for as long as needed to handle your inquiry and maintain the commercial relationship, unless you request its deletion.

sections.title: 6. Your rights
body: You may request access to, correction of, deletion of, or portability of your personal data, as well as object to or restrict its processing. To exercise any of these rights, email us at hola@geodot.app and we will respond within a reasonable time.

sections.title: 7. Changes to this policy
body: We may update this policy to reflect legal changes or changes in our practices. The current version will be posted on this page with its update date.

sections.title: 8. Contact
body: If you have questions about this policy or how we handle your data, email us at hola@geodot.app.

meta.title: Terms of Use | Geodot
meta.description: The terms and conditions governing use of the Geodot website.

hero.title: Terms of
hero.subtitle: The terms governing access to and use of this website.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of terms
body: By accessing and using this website you agree to these terms of use. If you do not agree, please do not use the site.

sections.title: 2. Use of the site
body: This site is provided for informational purposes about Geodot's products and services. You agree to use it lawfully and not to interfere with its operation, security, or availability.

sections.title: 3. Intellectual property
body: All content on the site (text, graphics, logos, images, and software) is owned by Geodot or its licensors and is protected by intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce or distribute it without prior written authorization.

sections.title: 4. No warranties
body: The site is provided "as is" and "as available." We do not warrant that the content is error-free or that the site will operate uninterrupted. Information on the site does not constitute a binding offer or professional advice.

sections.title: 5. Limitation of liability
body: To the extent permitted by law, Geodot shall not be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from the use of, or inability to use, this site.

sections.title: 6. Third-party links
body: The site may contain links to third-party sites. We do not control and are not responsible for their content or privacy practices.

sections.title: 7. Changes to these terms
body: We may modify these terms at any time. The version in effect will be the one posted on this page with its update date.

sections.title: 8. Contact
body: For any questions about these terms, email us at hola@geodot.app.

hero.title: Everything you want to know
hero.subtitle: The questions operations, IT, and finance teams ask us most. Straight answers, with numbers, no fluff.

items.q: What exactly does the platform include?
items.a: WMS, TMS, smart routing, load planning, Control Tower, cold chain, document manager, and yard/dock scheduling modules. Everything connected in one cloud-native platform: start with the modules you need and add the rest whenever you want.

items.q: Can I really load trucks to 100%?
items.a: Yes. We calculate load planning and routing at the same time, so we know exactly what fits in the truck before we route. A regional bottler went from 85% to 99% load and cut transport cost by more than 8%.

items.q: Our operation is very specific. Will you understand it?
items.a: That is exactly why we exist. Geodot was built from real operations: returnables, FEFO per customer, shelf-life per customer, cold chain, bonded warehouses. It is highly configurable, with over 300 parameters per SKU.

items.q: Do I need to change my hardware?
items.a: No. We work with standard Android, your existing Zebra scanners, and any GPS provider. You do not have to buy new equipment to get started.

items.q: What uptime does the platform offer?
items.a: 99.9% availability. It is cloud-native, with real-time visibility and traceability from day one.
Basic routing in 4 to 6 weeks. WMS plus routing in 2 to 3 months. Full platform in 3 to 4 months. Results in 90 days, not a year.

items.q: Do I have to replace my ERP?
items.a: No. Geodot powers your ERP, it does not replace it. We integrate via APIs with SAP, BASIS, and Oracle, reaching a level of detail (pack, pallet, location, load plan) that even SAP S/4HANA does not.

items.q: What about the knowledge living in our expert's head?
items.a: We document and automate it. Load-planning and operating rules are codified per SKU, so you no longer depend on one person being there to build the load.
Guided implementation by a team that has lived the operation. Native-language support, with presence in Mexico and across LATAM.

items.q: Which systems do you integrate with?
items.a: SAP (TM/EWM), Oracle (OTM), BASIS (Release 53), local SQL systems, refrigeration telemetry, billing systems, and even TXT files. We do it through our own APIs.

items.q: Connecting to BASIS or SAP is usually a headache. How do you handle it?
items.a: With our own RESTful APIs and real experience integrating Coca-Cola bottlers on BASIS Release 53. The integration is deep, not a patch on top.

items.q: Does it replace SAP TM or EWM?
items.a: No. It powers them. SAP remains your system of record; Geodot adds the logistics execution layer (routing + load planning in real time) that those suites do not solve together.

Yes. We integrate temperature sensors for 100% traceability and early alerts: you find out about an excursion when it happens, not when the truck gets rejected. Differentiated slots and rules for cold and dry product.
SaaS subscription based on number of users, operation volume, and contracted modules. Custom models are available. We quote after the demo.

Usually under 12 months. The real results we've seen in operation: truck load from 85% to 99%, 8% lower transport cost, and 4 to 5 fewer trucks a day from the returnable-glass rule. For your case we calculate it with your own data in the demo, no made-up numbers.

items.q: Do you offer support in Spanish?
items.a: Yes. We are a regional company with presence in Mexico, Argentina, Colombia, and Ecuador. The whole team is native Spanish-speaking.

items.q: What size of operation makes sense?
items.a: Medium to large operations with intensive physical logistics: from dozens to hundreds of trucks and hundreds of SKUs. If your operation is 1 to 5 vehicles with no IT team, we are probably not the right fit yet.

cta.title: Still have a question?
cta.subtitle: Book a 30-minute demo and we will answer with your operation on the table.

hero.title: We power your ERP,
hero.subtitle: Geodot integrates with your systems through its own APIs and adds the logistics execution layer your ERP cannot solve. No rip-and-replace, in production in 90 days.

how.title: Your ERP stays
how.description: Geodot does not compete with SAP, Oracle, or BASIS. It connects on top and adds real-time routing and load planning, at a level of detail those suites do not reach.

points.title: Connects, doesn't replace
points.body: Your ERP keeps master data, inventory, and billing. Geodot reads and writes via API, without touching your core or breaking what already works.

points.title: Reaches the fine detail
points.body: Pack, pallet, location, load plan, shelf-life per customer. An operational level even SAP S/4HANA does not cover, with no ERP customization required.

points.title: In production in 90 days
points.body: Basic routing in 4 to 6 weeks, full platform in 3 to 4 months. Measurable results from the first month, not after a year-long project.

systems.title: Deep integration,
systems.description: From global suites to local systems and field telemetry. If your data exists, we connect it.

items.name: SAP (TM / EWM)
items.detail: API integration with SAP Transportation and Extended Warehouse. Geodot runs the routing and load planning SAP does not solve together.

items.name: Oracle (OTM)
items.detail: Connection with Oracle Transportation Management to sync orders, routes, and deliveries in real time.

items.name: BASIS (Release 53)
items.detail: Proven integration with the core ERP of Coca-Cola bottlers, including returnables and load-margin rules.

items.name: Local systems (SQL)
items.detail: Read and write against your own SQL databases and in-house builds, without migrating your data.

items.name: Refrigeration telemetry
items.detail: Temperature sensors for cold-chain traceability, early alerts, and 100% logging.

items.name: Billing and TXT files
items.detail: Connection with billing systems and flat-file (TXT) exchange when the legacy system requires it.

speed.title: 90 days, not 12 months
speed.body: While a traditional suite takes 6 to 12 months to launch, Geodot goes into production in 90 days on top of your current systems. No ERP replacement, no operational downtime.

cta.title: Want to validate the integration with your stack?
cta.subtitle: Book a technical session and we will show you how we connect to your ERP and field systems.

hero.title: Grow with us
hero.subtitle: A partner program for integrators, technology partners, and strategic allies who want to bring smart logistics to their customers across the region.

categories.title: Three ways to
categories.description: Choose the role that best fits your business. Every category gets access to the full tier program and the services model.

items.name: Channel Partner
items.body: You resell and implement Geodot with your own customers. Ideal for integrators and logistics consultancies with commercial presence in the region.

items.name: Technology Partner
items.body: You connect your product or technology with the platform. Built for hardware makers, ISVs, and telemetry providers that add value to the ecosystem.

items.name: Strategic Alliance
items.body: We build a long-term joint go-to-market. For allies who want to co-develop vertical or regional solutions with Geodot.

tiers.title: Three tiers,
tiers.description: Your tier is set by the annual business volume generated with Geodot. More volume means more benefits and better margin.

items.name: Gold
items.body: The entry point to the program. Access to sales materials, base training, and support for your first implementations.

items.name: Platinum
items.body: For established partners. Better commercial terms, priority technical support, and co-marketing.

items.name: Diamond
items.body: The highest tier. Preferred margin, joint roadmap, and positioning as a strategic Geodot ally in the region.

services.title: You choose
services.description: How implementation services are split depends on who executes. The more in-house capability you have, the more margin you keep.

items.title: Geodot executes: 30%
items.body: If Geodot performs the implementation and professional services, the partner keeps 30% of the services value. Ideal for starting without your own technical team.

items.title: GCIE-certified partner: 100%
items.body: If your team is certified as a Geodot Certified Implementation Expert (GCIE), you execute the services yourself and keep 100% of their value.

contact.title: Let's bring smart logistics to more customers
contact.subtitle: Tell us about your business and we will build the alliance plan that fits you best.
